reason: Response A is a single, tightly compressed aphorism that lands one sharp idea (organizations simulating progress while avoiding it), which fits Opus's tendency toward restraint and a crisp, original turn of phrase. Response B leans on the well-worn "minutes are kept and hours are lost" and "could have been an email" tropes and piles on a second sentence, reading more like the safer, more conventional Sonnet output. The economy and freshness of A point to my own authorship.
